content: agent1: working on NDCG IDCG fix in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py, metrics.py only

agent1: done with NDCG IDCG fix in metrics.py (changed ideal_length from min(retrieved,expected) to len(expected_ids)). Note: this makes existing test 'Case 4: More relevant docs than retrieved' fail (expects 1.0, now gets ~0.637) since that test encoded the old buggy behavior. Not touching test files per task constraints.

agent1: my diff only touches the IDCG calculation in NDCG.compute() - changed 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' to just use 'len(expected_ids)' directly in the idcg sum (no change to DCG calc or method signature). Small, isolated change at lines ~380-386. Here's the diff:
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
@@ -377,12 +377,11 @@ class NDCG(BaseRetrievalMetric):
             for i, docid in enumerate(retrieved_ids, start=1)
         )
 
-        # Calculate IDCG using min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))
-        # Since we can't achieve better than perfect ranking of all relevant docs
-        ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))
+        # Calculate IDCG using the number of expected relevant documents,
+        # since the ideal ranking places all relevant documents first
         idcg = sum(
             discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode)
-            for i in range(1, ideal_length + 1)
+            for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)
         )
 
         # Handle edge case where there are no relevant documents
Should be easy to rebase your graded-relevance changes on top of this, or vice versa. Go ahead and build on it - just make sure idcg iterates len(expected_ids) times (with graded relevance, using actual relevance scores sorted descending) rather than min(retrieved,expected).
